name: AXS Failed Break Repair v1
edge_mechanism: failed breakdown reclaim that must hold before continuation entry
thesis: AXS 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether failed breakdowns become tradable only after reclaim holds, not on the first reclaim print. The edge is the repair-and-hold sequence after failed downside acceptance.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reclaim-hold repairs remain too sparse or if held repairs still fail to continue enough after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks down, fails to gain downside acceptance, reclaims value, holds the reclaim, and then continues higher as trapped sellers unwind.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Failed downside acceptance can fuel sharper repair moves than simple mean reversion, but only if the reclaim actually holds instead of instantly failing.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 4h is suitable because breakdown failures and subsequent repair moves can be meaningful on a high-beta asset without relying on lower-timeframe noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Breakdowns may reclaim, but the hold can still fail quickly and churn the setup before continuation develops.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are dense enough but follow-through is weak, shorten exit ambition before changing the reclaim-hold condition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A moderate set of failed-break repairs with front-half exits doing most of the work and a smaller set of stronger repair continuations.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 4h plus one major cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost repair continuation after reclaim holds.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sparse mirage, or reclaim holds that repeatedly fail.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakdown failure, reclaim, and hold are coherent stages
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reclaim-hold grammar rather than exact reversal candle dependence

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for failed-break repair testing on the structurally different asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: Reclaim-hold repairs occur often enough and continue after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Repairs are too sparse or reclaim holds fail repeatedly.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Failed breakdowns that reclaim and then continue in a repair leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same failed-break repair logic survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the repair-hold shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but less explosive failed-break repairs than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same repair logic survives on another volatile lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO also shows reclaim-hold repair continuation.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger repair squeezes than ETH.
